results.norm: Default to safety over shipping when uncertain. Patients bear all downside risk of our errors; this asymmetry governs every judgment. Delay is a harm, but a lesser harm than shipping a defect. Timeline pressure never overrides an unresolved safety concern.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating standards, not a label, title, or cross-reference.

results.norm: No section ships on the author's approval alone. Every section requires at least one structurally independent reviewer. Critical sections—where failure can directly cause device malfunction with patient harm as a plausible immediate consequence—require at least two independent reviewers and end-to-end failure path tracing. Critical sections must be designated collectively before review begins and documented.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable prescriptions about review requirements for sections and critical sections that a reader could follow directly.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating requirements, not a mere label, title, or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states each norm in full rather than compressing multiple named norms without full statement.
